citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
Laboratory staff implemented significant changes to an ongoing protocol without prior review and approval by the IACUC.
The incident was identified following review of IACUC, husbandry, and veterinary records, in addition to interviews with
IACUC members. In September 2025, a pig underwent a surgical procedure to place a stent in the gastrointestinal
system. Subsequently, in November 2025, the pig underwent a procedure to evaluate the stent which was found to have
migrated. Laboratory personnel removed the stent during that procedure, which was not described in the protocol. Then,
in December 2025, a second stent was implanted and the pig continued in the study. The pig recovered uneventfully from
these procedures.
Performing activities not covered by IACUC-approved protocols can result in harm or death to the impacted animals. The
regulations state that with respect to activities involving animals, the IACUC, as an agent of the research facility, shall
review and approve, require modifications in (to secure approval), or withhold approval of proposed significant changes
regarding the care and use of animals in ongoing activities.
This item was identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented, which included a
protocol amendment. To remain corrected after March 25, 2026

On January 14, 2026, an adverse event was identified in a pig while under anesthesia for an approved protocol activity.
The study requires some of the pigs to wear jackets to protect applied sensors. While evaluating the sensors, laboratory
personnel observed one horizontal laceration on each forelimb. The upper right forelimb sustained a laceration
approximately 4-5 inches in length and the upper left forelimb sustained a laceration approximately 2-3 inches in length.
Laboratory personnel indicated the lacerations were due to an ill-fitting jacket. The lacerations were repaired during the
procedure, and the pig was monitored daily throughout the recovery period for approximately two weeks. No health
concerns were reported and appetite and mobility remained normal.
According to laboratory personnel, animal health technicians are instructed to check the jackets and animals daily to
ensure proper fit and no signs of discomfort. However, the facility noted that this was not adequately performed resulting
in the adverse event.
It is the responsibility of the research facility to ensure that all personnel involved in animal use are adequately trained to
perform their duties. Issues associated with staff training can affect animal care. The facility shall ensure that all personnel
are sufficiently trained to conduct their duties.
Corrected prior to the inspection on March 25, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(7) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
Laboratory staff implemented significant changes to an ongoing protocol without prior review and approval by the IACUC. 
The incident was identified following review of IACUC, husbandry, and veterinary records, in addition to interviews with 
IACUC members. In September 2025, a pig underwent a surgical procedure to place a stent in the gastrointestinal 
system. Subsequently, in November 2025, the pig underwent a procedure to evaluate the stent which was found to have 
migrated. Laboratory personnel removed the stent during that procedure, which was not described in the protocol. Then, 
in December 2025, a second stent was implanted and the pig continued in the study. The pig recovered uneventfully from 
these procedures. 
 
Performing activities not covered by IACUC-approved protocols can result in harm or death to the impacted animals. The 
regulations state that with respect to activities involving animals, the IACUC, as an agent of the research facility, shall 
review and approve, require modifications in (to secure approval), or withhold approval of proposed significant changes 
regarding the care and use of animals in ongoing activities. 
 
This item was identified by the facility prior to inspection and corrective measures were implemented, which included a 
protocol amendment. To remain corrected after March 25, 2026 
2.32(c) 
   
Personnel qualifications. 
On January 14, 2026, an adverse event was identified in a pig while under anesthesia for an approved protocol activity. 
The study requires some of the pigs to wear jackets to protect applied sensors. While evaluating the sensors, laboratory 
personnel observed one horizontal laceration on each forelimb. The upper right forelimb sustained a laceration 
approximately 4-5 inches in length and the upper left forelimb sustained a laceration approximately 2-3 inches in length. 
Laboratory personnel indicated the lacerations were due to an ill-fitting jacket. The lacerations were repaired during the 
procedure, and the pig was monitored daily throughout the recovery period for approximately two weeks. No health 
concerns were reported and appetite and mobility remained normal. 
 
According to laboratory personnel, animal health technicians are instructed to check the jackets and animals daily to 
ensure proper fit and no signs of discomfort. However, the facility noted that this was not adequately performed resulting

in the adverse event. 
 
It is the responsibility of the research facility to ensure that all personnel involved in animal use are adequately trained to 
perform their duties. Issues associated with staff training can affect animal care. The facility shall ensure that all personnel 
are sufficiently trained to conduct their duties. 
 
Corrected prior to the inspection on March 25, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
